Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: I need to group these events into cases based on the attributes provided The main challenge is that there is no explicit case ID so I have to infer it using the sequence of activities timing and other attributes like patient age gender department etc

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: First I will start by looking at the timestamps The events are all on the same day March 15 2024 so the date is the same for all The times range from 080513Z to 084013Z That is about 35 minutes total Since they are all within a short timeframe maybe cases are interleaved in time

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Next looking at the activities Each case should have a sequence of activities that make sense For example Register patient is usually the first step Then Initial triage if it is in the ER Then maybe Doctor consultation X ray imaging Lab test requested etc So each case should have a logical flow

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now the departments ER Surgery Radiology Laboratory Some activities happen in specific departments For example Register patient can be in ER or Surgery as seen in events 1 2 3 But event 3 is in Surgery department Maybe that is a scheduled surgery patient registered in Surgery

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Patient age and gender might help group events Let us check Patient 45 M events 1 4 7 12 15 Patient 62 F events 2 5 8 11 14 17 Patient 28 M events 3 6 9 13 16 18 So there are three distinct patient groups based on age and gender

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Starting with the 45 M patient Event 1 Register patient in ER Event 4 Initial triage in ER Event 7 X ray in Radiology Event 12 Doctor consultation in ER Event 15 Discharged from ER That seems like a logical flow registration triage imaging consultation discharge So that is one case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Next the 62 F patient Event 2 Register in ER Event 5 Initial triage in ER Event 8 Lab test requested by Dr Smith in ER Event 11 Doctor consultation in ER Event 14 Prescription in ER Event 17 Discharged from ER This also makes sense They registered had triage lab test doctor visit got a prescription and discharged So Case 2

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then the 28 M patient Event 3 Register in Surgery maybe scheduled surgery Event 6 Doctor consultation in Surgery scheduled knee surgery Event 9 Surgery preparation in Surgery Event 13 Surgery performed in Surgery Event 16 Recovery room in Surgery Event 18 Discharged from Surgery This seems like a scheduled surgery case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait but event 3 is in Surgery department Maybe the registration for a scheduled surgery is done in Surgery department That makes sense

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Checking for overlaps the 28 year old events are interleaved with others but distinguishable by patient attributes Also event 3 is in Surgery while the other registrations are in ER so the 28 year old is a separate case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The three cases are Case 1 45 M ER then Radiology back to ER discharge Case 2 62 F ER discharge Case 3 28 M Surgery preparation surgery recovery discharge

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me double check each event Case 1 1 4 7 12 15 yes all match Case 2 2 5 8 11 14 17 yes Case 3 3 6 9 13 16 18 yes No events are left out So this grouping makes sense
